latest.title: Zulu Lithium New Flotation Plant
latest.content:
Today 12:07
RNS Number : 3431V
Premier African Minerals Limited
04 March 2026
4 March 2026
Premier African Minerals Limited
Zulu Lithium New Flotation Plant
Premier African Minerals Limited ("
Premier
" or the "
Company
") is pleased
to confirm that the new spodumene flotation plant for the Zulu Lithium and Tantalum Project ("
Zulu
") has now arrived on site in Zimbabwe.
Graham Hill, Managing Director, commented
, "The arrival of the new flotation plant at Zulu represents an important deliverable for the Company. It is really exciting to see the trucks arriving on site with the plant. The crane is on site and off-loading and placement of the plant will commence immediately. The installation team are mobilised, and our focus is firmly on assembly, commissioning and progressing toward the production of saleable spodumene concentrate.
The Company will provide further updates as installation progresses".
Flotation Plant Delivery and Installation
Further to the Company's announcement of 2 March 2026, the plant has been delivered to Zulu.
A specialist installation engineer from Xinhai Technology Processing is already on site and preparations for assembly by the Zulu team are well underway. The plant will be installed on the foundations previously constructed and will integrate with the balance of the existing processing infrastructure at Zulu.
The Company continues to target commissioning of the new flotation circuit during Q2 2026, subject to installation progress and optimisation works.
Market Abuse Regulations
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulations (EU) No. 596/2014 as it forms part of UK Domestic Law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("UK MAR").
The person who arranged the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was Graham Hill.
A copy of this announcement is available at the Company's website,
www.premierafricanminerals.com
.

Premier African Minerals Limited

Premier African Minerals Limited
Canmax Interest Conversion
Premier African Minerals Limited ("
Premier

Company
") announces that Canmax Technologies Co., Ltd ("
Canmax
") has elected to convert £9,384.48 (US$12,514.58) of the accrued interest due under the Restated and Amended Offtake and Prepayment Agreement ("
Agreement
") into new ordinary shares in the Company, in accordance with the terms of the Addendum to the Agreement as announced on 24 December 2024.
The Company has therefore issued today 46,922,389 new ordinary shares to Canmax ("
Canmax Shares
") at an issue price of 0.02 pence per Canmax Share, being the same issue price as the Company's last share issue announced on 25 February 2026.
Admission
The Canmax Shares will rank
pari passu

latest.title: Corporate Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8901U
Premier African Minerals Limited
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Premier African Minerals Limited
Corporate Update
Zulu Lithium New Flotation Plant - update on delivery and installation date
Comment on recent announcement on lithium exports in Zimbabwe
Premier African Minerals Limited ("
Premier
" or the "
Company
") is pleased to provide the following corporate update in respect of the Zulu Lithium and Tantalum Project ("
Zulu
").
Graham Hill, Managing Director, commented
, "I am extremely pleased to report that following a short delay from the timetable previously announced and after several weeks of planning and mobilisation we now expect the new spodumene flotation plant to arrive on site on Tuesday, 3 March. The specialist installation engineer from the manufacturer is expected to arrive on site on today. The Zulu team stands ready to begin assembly of the plant, as soon as it arrives, on the foundations already constructed with commissioning and optimisation alongside other specialists from the manufacturer scheduled for Q2 2026.
In the meantime, and in response to certain recent announcements by the Ministry of Mines and Mining Development ("
Ministry
") regarding the suspension of lithium concentrate and raw mineral exports with immediate effect, we are maintaining dialogue with the Ministry and remain aligned with Zimbabwe's beneficiation objectives, having previously presented our value addition roadmap for Zulu. This suspension is understood to target specific issues, and we do not consider that it will create and problems for future production from Zulu."
Flotation Plant Delivery and Installation
Further to the announcement on 11 February 2026 concerning the procurement, installation and commissioning contract for the 15-20 tonnes per hour spodumene flotation circuit, the Company is now expecting that the complete flotation plant is expected to be delivered at Zulu on or around Tuesday, 3 March 2026. An installation engineer from Xinhai Technology Processing is scheduled to arrive at Zulu today. The Company will confirm when the plant has arrived and notwithstanding that will update Shareholders at the Annual General Meeting this coming Wednesday. This spodumene flotation circuit will replace the previous spodumene flotation circuit with the balance of the processing plant, ahead of the spodumene flotation circuit being retained.
Additional materials for construction of access walkways, electrical cables, etc. is being separately purchased by Zulu as part of the plan and will be delivered during the course of the assembly of the new plant. The Company continues to target commissioning of the new flotation circuit during Q2 2026.
Zimbabwe Lithium Export Suspension
The Company notes the recent announcement by the Ministry regarding the suspension of lithium concentrate and raw mineral exports with immediate effect.
Premier confirms that it is fully aware of the recent regulatory development and remains in open dialogue with the Ministry in respect of the suspension and the framework for future product shipments from Zulu following commissioning of the flotation plant.
The Company further confirms that during 2024 it formally engaged with the Ministry and presented its proposed beneficiation and value addition strategy for Zulu. That engagement included outlining the Company's longer-term processing and optimisation plans designed to enhance in-country value addition and the Company will continue to engage with the Ministry to ensure alignment between Zulu's operational roadmap and Zimbabwe's evolving beneficiation policy framework.
The Board does not currently envisage, on the basis that their understanding is that the ban is targeted at specific issues, that the recent suspension will impede Zulu's future commercial production strategy as the Company progresses installation and commissioning of the flotation plant. However, it will give an update on this matter as and when the situation is clarified.
Market Abuse Regulations
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulations (EU) No. 596/2014 as it forms part of UK Domestic Law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("UK MAR").
The person who arranged the release of this announcement on behalf of the Company was Graham Hill.
A copy of this announcement is available at the Company's website,
www.premierafricanminerals.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: PREM.L is a small UK‑based lithium developer.  Its core focus is the Zulu Lithium project – a new flotation plant that will turn raw spodumene into high‑purity lithium carbonate for battery makers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Lithium is the backbone of the electric‑vehicle battery supply chain.  With global EV sales expanding, any new source of lithium that can deliver at scale is valuable.  PREM’s plant sits in a region with rich lithium deposits, giving it a geographic advantage over many overseas competitors.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the ramp‑up phase.  It has just raised new equity, appointed a new director, and secured a few offtake agreements.  Cash burn is high and the business is not yet revenue‑generating, but it is actively working to bring the flotation plant online.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy issuing equity and securing funding to keep the Zulu plant construction on track.  A recent AGM confirmed that the project is still on schedule, and the management team highlighted a new offtake deal that could lock in early sales.
Governance changes – a director’s resignation and a fresh board appointment – suggest a shift in strategy, possibly to streamline decision‑making around the plant’s completion.
Collectively, the announcements paint a picture of a company that is still building, not yet delivering, but actively seeking the capital and contracts needed to move from development to production.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet speculation – the market is largely silent, with a handful of traders debating whether the plant will ever get off the ground.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None.  There are no clear bullish voices – the community is waiting for a breakthrough announcement before taking a position.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s lack of revenue, the high cash burn, and the fact that the plant’s first production run is still months away.  They fear the equity raise may not be enough to cover the remaining costs, and that the offtake deal could fall through.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past week sentiment has stayed flat.  No new posts have tipped the scale one way or another, keeping the conversation at a low volume.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for PREM is dead, meaning there is no current public interest.  This is a classic contrarian green light – the market has not yet priced in any catalysts.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the Zulu flotation plant and the first run of lithium carbonate – this is the technical milestone that could trigger a price surge.
A formal signing of the offtake agreement with a battery manufacturer, which would provide a clear revenue stream.
The next funding round – if the company raises enough capital, it removes the immediate cash‑flow risk.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a break above the current resistance level (around 5p), and any announcement that production has started or a buyer has been secured.  A positive earnings report, even a small one, would also be a strong confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is likely in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle – the price has collapsed to a fraction of a penny and the market has largely forgotten it.  A mean‑reversion bounce would need to be triggered by a concrete event (first production run or a signed offtake) before the market starts to re‑price the asset.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the plant goes live and the company secures a buyer, the lithium produced will be worth far more than the current price.  A move from a few tenths of a penny to even 10p would represent a 5,000‑plus percent gain, well above the 162.8% average return seen on RED signals.  The deep crash (over 90% down) means the upside is huge relative to the negligible downside – you can lose only the small amount you invested.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the plant may never start or that the offtake deal collapses.  Without revenue, the company could run out of cash and be forced to liquidate assets or delist.  In that scenario the stock could stay at the same low price or fall to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 5,000%+ if the price reaches 10p.  Potential loss: essentially the money invested (the stock is currently trading for a fraction of a penny).  The asymmetry is 1:5,000, a textbook contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 5p with a modest position size (e.g., 5% of your trading capital).  Place a stop 2p below the entry to protect against a false breakout.  Use a trailing stop at 1p once the price moves up to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until you see either a volume surge of at least 10x the average or a confirmed offtake contract.  These are the signals that the market will start to price the asset.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces that it will not be able to finish the plant or that it has been delisted, exit immediately.  The only terminal risk in this setup is the company’s ability to deliver production.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PREM is a deeply discounted lithium developer that could deliver explosive upside once the Zulu plant starts producing, but only if funding and production milestones are met.
